paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree
summary: Question 4.2 defines the directed chromatic threshold ε_H for a fixed digraph H as the minimum ε ≥ 0 such that digraphs with sufficiently high chromatic number and minimum out-degree at least ε|V(D)| must contain H as a subdigraph. The source paper itself settles the ε_H = 0 case for orientations of cycles (Theorem 1.3); the general question for arbitrary digraphs H remains open. A September 2025 follow-up (arXiv:2509.07760) studies the closely related notion of chromatic profiles δ_χ^+(H, k) for oriented cycles, proving exact thresholds for transitive tournaments and specific oriented cycles, and explicitly cites the source paper—but does not resolve Question 4.2 in general.

since_posted.title: A directed Andrásfai-Erdős-Sós theorem and chromatic profiles of oriented cycles
since_posted.claim: Introduces and studies chromatic profiles δ_χ^+(H, k) for oriented cycles—a refinement of the directed chromatic threshold—proving exact out-degree thresholds for transitive tournaments and several oriented cycles; cites arXiv:2503.20045 for the ε_H = 0 characterisation but does not resolve ε_H for general digraphs H.

search_queries: Koerts Moore Spirkl "directed chromatic threshold" digraph 2503.20045
directed chromatic threshold digraph "minimum out-degree" chromatic number subdigraph 2025 2026
notes: Question 4.2 is a broad open research question rather than a specific conjecture: it asks for ε_H for every fixed digraph H. The source paper itself resolves the ε_H = 0 case for oriented cycles. The closely related chromatic profile δ_χ^+(H, k) studied in arXiv:2509.07760 provides partial progress on the structure of the threshold for specific oriented cycles. The source paper was published in Combinatorica (Springer, 2026, doi:10.1007/s00493-026-00207-0).
